title: Triggers, Sibling Math, Contours, Content-Sizing & Commands
description: Five very new browser-native primitives: discrete scroll-trigger animations, sibling-index() self-arranging layout, a shared border/text contour, one-line auto-growing fields, and zero-JS Invoker Commands.

drops.title: Threshold Burst Triggers
drops.description: Milestone badges fire once when crossing a tunable activation line, then reverse cleanly on scroll-up.
drops.concept: The new CSS animation-trigger and timeline-trigger properties let a discrete, one-shot animation fire when scroll crosses an activation range, with separate forward/backward play actions — unlike continuous scroll-scrub timelines. This demo feature-detects native support and falls back to an IntersectionObserver-style rAF scroll listener that fires the identical pop-and-particle-burst effect once per direction change.
drops.source: CSS-Tricks — "A First Look at Scroll-Triggered Animations"

interaction.mouse: Scroll to cross the activation line; drag the line slider to retune
interaction.touch: Scroll on mobile fires the same triggers

drops.title: Self-Arranging Card Deck
drops.description: A radial card deck that recomputes every card's angle live via sibling-index() and sibling-count() — no JS layout math.
drops.concept: sibling-index() and sibling-count() are new CSS functions that let each element read its own position and total sibling count directly in a calc() expression, enabling fully self-arranging layouts (radial fans, evenly spaced rings, auto hue distribution) with zero JavaScript recalculation when children are added or removed. This demo applies the native calc() formula behind @supports and mirrors the exact same math in JS as a fallback for browsers without support yet.
drops.source: Smashing Magazine — "Mathematical Layouts With sibling-index() And sibling-count()"
tech: JS fallback mirroring the same formula

interaction.mouse: Drag radius/rotation sliders, click +/- to add or remove cards
interaction.click: Add/remove buttons rebalance the ring instantly

drops.title: Contour-Hugging Type
drops.description: A card whose border and body paragraph share one sampled hand-drawn contour instead of a generic circle or blob.
drops.concept: A single set of sampled points defines a custom left-edge contour (scallop, wave, or jagged), which is fed to two CSS features at once: border-shape reshapes the actual box border along that exact path where supported, and shape-outside carves a matching polygon exclusion zone so the paragraph text reflows around the identical silhouette. Where border-shape isn't yet supported, an absolutely positioned SVG path stroke redraws the same contour as the visible border.

interaction.mouse: Switch contour style buttons, drag amplitude slider

drops.title: Content-Sized Fields
drops.description: A chat composer, tag input, and search box that all grow or shrink to fit their own content in one line of CSS.
drops.concept: field-sizing: content makes input, textarea, and select elements size themselves to their live content instead of a fixed cols/rows — width for single-line fields, height for textarea, bounded purely by min-height/max-height in lh units. This removes the need for the common JS pattern of measuring scrollHeight on every input event. A JS fallback mirrors the same clamped growth for browsers without support.
drops.source: Kalan — "CSS field-sizing: Auto-Resize Form Elements with One Line of CSS"

interaction.mouse: Type in the composer/tag/search fields; drag min/max sliders
interaction.click: Enter adds a tag, × removes one
interaction.touch: Fields grow correctly on mobile keyboards

drops.title: Declarative Invoker Commands
drops.description: A dialog, two steppers and two flip-cards wired entirely with command/commandfor attributes instead of per-element JS listeners.
drops.concept: The HTML Invoker Commands API adds command and commandfor attributes to buttons, letting them declaratively control a target element's behaviour. Built-in commands like show-modal and close need zero JavaScript at all. Custom commands (prefixed --) fire a single generic 'command' event on the target, so one document-level listener can drive an unlimited number of steppers or toggles instead of binding a click handler per button.

interaction.mouse: Click Open dialog, stepper +/-, and flip buttons
